name: CPG hybrid — fitness v6 (symmetric contact penalty, strong alternation)
changes_from_exp005: Replaced W_AIRBORNE (airborne penalty) with W_SYM_CONTACT=3.0 (symmetric bilateral contact penalty)
W_ALTERNATION: 1.5 → 8.0 — alternation bonus now competitive with speed_bonus (~960 pts/ep for walking)
W_SPEED_BONUS: 2.0 → 1.0 — moderate walking speed earns bonus; hopping not 4× more valuable
MIN_SPEED: 1.0 → 0.5 m/s — lowered threshold so steady walking always earns speed bonus
Fresh start with sigma=0.5 (not warm from exp004 hopping attractor — warm from exp003 stride)

fitness_function.formula: cumulative_distance*30 + upright*0.2 + speed_bonus*1.0 + alternation_bonus*8.0 + time_alive*0.05 - pitch_rate*0.05 - sym_contact_penalty*3.0 - joint_penalty*0.01 - impact_penalty*0.005 - fall_penalty*200

rationale.exp005_diagnosis: W_ALTERNATION=1.5 was too weak vs speed_bonus (~2000 pts for hopper). CMA-ES correctly ignored alternation. Airborne penalty also easily exploited — hopper keeps one foot lightly touching while launching.
rationale.sym_contact_penalty: Detects hopping via bilateral force symmetry at landing. Formula: penalty += 1 - |Fr-Fl|/(Fr+Fl+eps) when both feet loaded. A hopper lands symmetrically (~1.0 penalty) every stride; a walker always has one dominant foot (~0.0 penalty). This makes hopping structurally impossible to exploit.
rationale.strong_alternation: W_ALTERNATION 1.5→8.0 means a walking gait (~120 switches/ep) earns ~960 pts. Combined with sym_contact_penalty=-900 for hopper, walking should now dominate CMA-ES's landscape.
rationale.warm_from_exp003: exp003 produced striding (not hopping) but fell. Starting from this rather than exp004 avoids the hopping attractor bias.

predicted_score_hopper.note: Hopper at 3 m/s but penalised heavily
